document.title: Photo Bridge Product Requirements
document.source: UI/product audit of src-ui routes, CLI transfer/convert flows, and Pixel 1 + Google Photos use case

product.name: Photo Bridge
product.summary: Local macOS app that prepares iOS media for Google Pixel 1 compatibility and ships it over ADB so the official Google Photos app on that device can back up at original quality under Google's grandfathered Pixel unlimited policy.
product.positioning: Local media prep + ADB courier for a real Pixel. Not a cloud uploader, not a Google Photos client, not a storage-limit bypass.
product.primaryPersona: Someone with a large iOS photo library who owns (or can borrow) an original Pixel-class device with unlimited original-quality Google Photos backup, and needs a reliable batch pipeline on tiny device storage.

withinTos: Transfer media via USB/ADB to device storage (e.g. /sdcard/DCIM/Camera)
Rely on the official Google Photos app on the device for backup
Keep marketing language as prepare + load a Pixel so the official app can back up
outOfScopeOrDisallowed: Spoofing Pixel identity to Google servers
Unofficial Google Photos APIs or modified clients
Automating Google account login or Photos web upload from the Mac
Claiming unlimited Google Photos without the physical grandfathered Pixel path

coreChainToday.capability: Size/count/month batches for tiny Pixel 1 storage
Repair capture dates for Google Photos sorting

ralphLoop.name: Ralph loop
ralphLoop.description: The recommended end-to-end product loop for Photo Bridge: recycle limited Pixel 1 disk by repeatedly preparing a batch that fits free space, pushing it to the device, waiting for official Google Photos backup, then purging the camera roll and repeating until the library is done.
ralphLoop.why: Pixel 1 free space is the bottleneck, not conversion. The app is already a strong toolkit; what is missing is orchestration of fill → backup → purge so users do not invent the pipeline each time.

steps.title: Connect Pixel
steps.detail: USB debugging authorized, device unlocked, connection status green.

steps.title: Measure free space
steps.detail: Read device free space and reserve a safety margin so a push cannot fill the disk to zero.

steps.title: Choose source library/folder
steps.detail: User picks an iOS export or intermediate folder.

steps.title: Convert / remux as needed
steps.detail: Make media Pixel-compatible; prefer smart defaults (remux when required).

steps.title: Open output folder after success
steps.detail: Reveal the sibling _Remuxed / _Copied (or next-available) folder so the user can verify and feed the next step without hunting paths.

steps.title: Shape batch to free space
steps.detail: Optional split by size/count so Part N fits measured free space (e.g. ~70% of available).

steps.title: Space check before push
steps.detail: Sum bytes to push; block or warn hard if batch will not fit free space; refresh storage immediately before transfer.

steps.title: Push to DCIM/Camera
steps.detail: ADB push folder or files; surface progress and disconnect/partial-file recovery.

steps.title: Google Photos handoff (manual, on-device)
steps.detail: Checklist only: keep phone awake/charging, Wi-Fi, Original quality backup, wait until upload queue is empty. Do not automate Google login or cloud upload from the Mac.

steps.title: Purge camera roll (user-confirmed)
steps.detail: After backup is done, free device storage with explicit confirmation; then loop to measure-space for the next part.

ralphLoop.successMetric: User can move a large iOS library through multiple Pixel-sized batches without overfilling the device, losing track of output folders, or guessing when it is safe to clear the camera roll.
ralphLoop.uiImplication: Prefer a guided Backup run (Easy mode) that stitches existing routes/CLI, rather than only a flat tool list. Power UI remains for granular control.

items.title: Space check before push
items.why: Pixel 1 storage is tiny; a push that does not fit causes failed transfers, partial files, and wasted battery. Highest leverage safety gate in the Ralph loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Before push folder/files, app knows (or refreshes) device free bytes when connected
App estimates total bytes of the selected transfer set
If estimated size + safety margin exceeds free space: block push with a clear message, or require explicit override
If free space cannot be read: warn and allow user to proceed only with acknowledgment
Available storage card on Transfer stays in sync after refresh and after a completed push
likelySurfaces: src-cli push path / shell storage events

items.title: Open new folder after conversion success
items.why: Convert/copy write to sibling folders (_Remuxed, _Copied, numbered variants). Users lose the handoff into push/split if the output path is not revealed. Second highest friction after overfull pushes.
acceptanceCriteria: On successful convert or copy session, UI surfaces the output directory path
Primary action: Open / Reveal in Finder (macOS) for that folder
Optional secondary: Push this folder (or navigate to Transfer with that path staged)
Works for both file-list and directory batch inputs that produce a sibling output root
Does not open Finder on failure or empty/no-op sessions
likelySurfaces: CLI session/summary events that include output directory

items.title: Expose safe camera-roll purge in UI
items.why: CLI purge + purgePixelFiles exist; Manage Device description promises purge but UI does not expose it. Without purge, the Ralph loop cannot recycle Pixel disk.
acceptanceCriteria: Manage Device (and/or Transfer) offers Purge camera roll when connected
Confirmation requires intentional action (e.g. type-to-confirm or strong dialog with file count)
Shows path (PIXEL_CAMERA_DIR) and approximate file count before delete
Never auto-purges; never runs during an active transfer
Success refreshes file list and storage

items.title: Align marketing copy with HEIC behavior
items.why: src-web claims HEIC→JPG; convert image path is copy + date fix. Mismatch confuses users and prioritization.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing and README either document current still-image behavior accurately OR product ships optional HEIC→JPEG
No claim of JPG conversion unless implemented
likelySurfaces: optional: image processor + convert UI toggle

items.title: End-to-end Backup run wizard (Easy mode)
items.why: Stitches connect → space → convert → open output → fit batch → space-check → push → Photos checklist → purge into one linear flow.
acceptanceCriteria: User can complete one full Ralph loop cycle without using the sidebar as a checklist
Power routes remain available for advanced use

items.title: Mount activity feed on convert/transfer (or remove dead code)
items.why: ActivityFeed and LogViewer exist but are not used by routes; power-user transparency is half-built.

items.title: Show page descriptions under titles
items.why: Routes set pageDescription in staticData; chrome only shows title. Short Pixel-1 context is written but invisible.

items.title: Post-convert push handoff
items.why: Beyond Finder reveal: one-tap Push this result or Transfer with staged path.

items.title: Per-page selection (or clear store on navigate)
items.why: Shared useMediaStore bleeds folder selection across Convert / Split / Fix Dates.

items.title: Enable select individual files on Convert
items.why: File picker is implemented but disabled; empty state is folder-only.

items.title: Fix Dates stats + inspect UI
items.why: BETA page has empty second column and little feedback; dates matter for Google Photos timeline.

items.title: Split dry-run / preview plan
items.why: In-place moves are scary; show N parts and sizes before moving.

items.title: Storage-aware batch sizing from device free space
items.why: Recommend split size from measured free space to feed the Ralph loop.

items.title: Auto remux vs copy from codec/container

findings.evidence: Sidebar is a flat tool list
findings.impact: Users invent the Pixel 1 backup pipeline themselves

findings.evidence: staticData.pageDescription set; RootPageHeader only shows title
Sidebar disables every route while any operation runs
findings.impact: Cannot prep next batch while a push runs

findings.evidence: ActivityFeed / LogViewer never mounted on routes
/ redirects in Tauri else IndexPage placeholder

findings: Copy vs remux wording understates risk of renaming to .mp4 without remux
No reveal output folder or push-results handoff (addressed by P0 open-output-after-convert)
Empty second column; no stats panel
Should surface why dates matter for Google Photos timeline
No problem filters (missing date, HEIC, Live Photo, huge video)
Local-only; not linked to device roll
Connection card thin for first-time ADB failures
Description promises purge; UI does not expose it (P0 safe-camera-roll-purge-ui)
DEV-only local cache purge is not the user-facing need
No defaults for push destination, convert mode, split size, fix-dates write mode, recent folders, completion notifications

quickWinsTable.item: Open / reveal new folder after conversion success
Expose safe camera-roll purge in UI
Fix marketing vs HEIC behavior (code or copy)
End-to-end wizard or sticky Next step after convert → push
Mount activity feed on convert/transfer or delete dead code
Show page descriptions under titles; short why for Pixel 1
Reveal in Finder / open _Remuxed|_Copied after jobs (extends open-output)
Per-page selection or clear store on navigate

productDirectionOptions.title: Pixel 1 Backup Station identity
productDirectionOptions.detail: Optimize every screen for the Ralph loop (fill → backup → purge); de-emphasize generic media tools.

productDirectionOptions.title: Easy wizard vs power UI
productDirectionOptions.detail: Guided Backup run for the loop; keep current routes for granular control (see TASKS.md personas).

productDirectionOptions.title: Browse as hub
productDirectionOptions.detail: Flag issues → fix → convert → queue for push from Browse.

productDirectionOptions.title: Stay CLI-honest
productDirectionOptions.detail: UI remains orchestrator over pb; only hold workflow state CLI cannot (history, wizard step, checklists).

bottomLine: Photo Bridge is already a strong toolkit for iOS → Pixel 1 → official Google Photos backup. The Ralph loop is the product: storage-aware batches, push preflight, open output after convert, post-backup purge, and eventually a guided path. Ship space check before push and open output after conversion success first.
